We're going to construct a lie bialgebra object in the category filtered complex vector spaces. First, the vector space, then the filtration, then the operations. 

Let $\def\C{\mathbb{C}} U : \mathsf{Alg}_\C \to \mathsf{Vect}_\C$ be the forgetful functor. Define the commutator $U-^{\otimes 2} \to U$ as follows.$$\begin{align*} [\cdot,\cdot]_A : UA^{\otimes 2} &\to UA \\ [a,b] &= ab - ba\end{align*}$$We now define two functors as follows. $$
\begin{align*}
|\cdot| : \mathsf{Alg}_\C &\to \mathsf{Vect}_C & \overline{|\cdot|} : \mathsf{Alg}_\C &\to \mathsf{Vect}_C\\
A &\mapsto \mathrm{codcok}[\cdot,\cdot]_A & A &\mapsto \mathrm{codcok}\big(\mathrm{cok}[\cdot,\cdot]_A \circ U(!:\C \to A)\big)
\end{align*}
$$*the following diagram illustrates the cokernels. note we obtain transforms $U \to |\cdot|$ and $U \to \overline{|\cdot|}$.*
<iframe class="quiver-embed" src="https://q.uiver.app/#&embed" width="100%" height="200" style="border-radius: 8px; border: none;"></iframe>
Now, let  $D_p$ the $p$-punctured disk and $\C : \mathsf{Grp} \to \mathsf{Alg}_\C$ be the group algebra functor. We will work over the vector space $\overline{|\C\pi|}$, where $\pi$ is the fundamental group of $D_p$ with base point on the outer boundary component. Our filtration is is generated by the augmentation ideal of $\C\pi$. $$
\begin{gather*}
\mathcal{I} = \langle \gamma - 1 \,|\, \gamma \in \pi \rangle = \ker U(\epsilon:\C\pi \to \C)\\[2mm]
U \C \pi \hookleftarrow \mathcal{I} \hookleftarrow \mathcal{I}^2 \hookleftarrow \cdots
\end{gather*}
$$where $\mathcal{I}^n = \mathrm{im}( I^{\otimes n} \hookrightarrow UC\pi^{\otimes n} \xrightarrow{U\mu} UC\pi )$. But this is a filtration on $U\C\pi$, that is, a diagram of the following form.
<iframe class="quiver-embed" src="https://q.uiver.app/#&embed" width="100%" height="200" style="border-radius: 8px; border: none;"></iframe>
We conclude by taking the quotient filtration on $|A|$.

> [!info] lemma.
> The map $$ \begin{align*}
\mu(a \otimes b) = -\sum_{q \in a \cap b} \varepsilon_q|a_qb_q|\end{align*}$$
is a lie bracket on $|\C\pi|$
